column36: Hiroim was definitely the MVP here, and Gladiator Hulk. Keeping things cleared was a priority and the early draws were very much in my favor.  I want to go back and play this one again when I have more time.

Both games went way better than I expected. 1st game player 1 had 3 Hiroim Redeemed fueled by chimichangas and rescued 'victims". When Goblin 2099 came up he had 0 attack. 2nd attempt Player 2 had the best Ghost Rider 2099 deck I've ever had, with Infernal Chainsaw generating tons of attack (fueled by Shenanigans).

column36: Got Hiroim's Rare, and with some Rally Kree Warriors, I was hoarding Shards and Bystanders to empower my Hiroim ramp cards! The other side cleaned their build amazingly early on, and managed to score lots of points with sheer power alone, from Ghost Rider 2099 to Captain Mar-Vell's Shards and Bruce Banner's bigger hands. No Escapes and two powerful builds meant that the Old One could only Dream of ever waking up. Good stuff!~

column36: The plan was to make a mostly red Hiroim ramp and mostly blue shard sides, and use rider to farm vp. Almost worked, rider didnt show up. Shuffled strikes from the top 3 times with Yondu tho. I am low on vp, hope 22 turns is fast enough to score high.
This is a fun set of heroes. I got lucky that Hiroim worked as he did not my first game. Doom and Ghost Rider are very good heroes.

Went for shards on first attempt. Realized bystanders were very powerful engine, so I put in Ronan to help produce more bystanders and shards. Couldn’t complete the game any faster but ended with more VP. Fun and challenging set up.
Danger Sense managed the Scheme pretty well, and Infernal Chainsaw did very good as we were able to recruit it early. Fun setup, and we will try it again after the tournament with other Heroes.
Tough setup and Adam Warlock was too slow first game. Hiroim did heavy lifting in getting a win.

The hero teams really worked out here. Felt like I could have shaved some time here or there but in game one, the full focus came with getting the Ghost Rider rare early and powering it home to get advantage. 

The Hiroim and Venompool deck was able to get bystanders early and use that to really inflate the victory points and help make both decks equal in terms of victory points. It is always fun playing Hiroim when he has a structure deck to work with.

Had a lot of fun with this one. Game 1 did not go as planned and I was only able to KO the Scheme MM after Twist 7 and never touched Annihulus. I switched up my heroes and Game 2 went a lot better. I tried to make a draw deck and a Smash deck, but the Smash deck wasn’t able to recruit enough cards to take off. However the draw deck went off with 3 back to back turns of drawing the entire deck and almost cleaning the board each time. Really enjoyed the combo of Rick Jones’ transformed rare and Ant-Man’s uncommon.

column36: Most fun setup we’ve played in quite a while. We raise a glass (of melk) this week

1st game got twists early so had a 21 attack god emperor to take down but minimum threat from villains. Second game was opposite where there was a real threat of escape early but only had to take down an 11 attack god emperor. Overall, really enjoyed the set up. Heroes were awesome combination of KO and draw abilities.

We had a lot of fun, but had to slow down and really plan out who, what and when to hit every body after losing the first attempt on what was going to be the last turn in a win...countdown schemes are a cruel mistress....Smashed our way to victory though. Pretty sure we're in top 3 with no escapes, although it was close a few times lol. Back to back twists were helpful in not moving the city. and hitting stationary targets

column36: Wow. Really happy with my game and I don't think I'd be as lucky again so I called it quits as soon as I could. On turn 11, after only a single scheme twist had occured, I had exactly 9 attack to get God-Emperor Doom out of the way right away. I felt like it could have been a bit faster taking down Annihilus, but I did manage to rescue plenty of bystanders along the way thanks to Totally Awesome Hulk and Galactus going back to the city for 7 bystanders with Annihilus' tactic. I unfortunately couldn't get Galactus back before the end of the game, but I think saving about two turns was probably worth it. Very tough setup but I feel like I got insanely lucky

I'm not a huge fan of momentum as a keyword. I find it hard to keep track of especially with other things going on. With this setup, there wasn't a lot of other things to track so it wasn't too bad. The first game had much stronger decks but a few early escapes hit me hard so I choose to submit a longer game but with no escapes. Having Amadeus plus ant man plus hulkbuster made an outwit draw deck super strong once it got rolling.

always fun trying a new scheme, manage to get god emperor doom, but never touched Annihilus, so lost a lot of points that way. player one went tech deck with Banner and Ant-Man, and after Doom killed Annihilus they were like 3 attack short of hitting him every single turn! player two went red deck with No-NAme and Hiroim, with some hulk assistance and finally got enough attack to smash puny doom!

This was a good time. Multiple Man plus undercover heroes meant plenty of deck thinning. We both enjoyed playing the SHIELD expansion and this game delivered.

column36: Super fun! We added Phil Coulson to enhance the shield levels and synergize with yelena and Deathlock. We added Gamora to add synergy with Starlord and potentially utilize "galactic assassin" to negate shards on an infinity gem. The villain deck was kind to us in that the first few scheme twists came out early on and did not bring out any infinity gems. This was a great jump start. Cool set up and very enjoyable match!
Great set up, Thanks. Our first game we got early escapes with a tough adversary draw, second game was a bit nicer, but lower undercovers for our points total.

Great setup - I'd enjoy this setup more if it wasn't a Villains game - just not used to the dark side rules. Just not used to KOing all wounds. Even so, fun setup with undercover and shards packing serious punch. Gamora was key for me. Her rare cancelling villain shards allowed me to shard dump. Even so, there were 4 stones in the city when it ended. Almost forgot - I went double SHIELD for starters for Deathlok and Coulson.

I got a few lucky plot twists early on that effectively did nothing, so that helped me get off to a good start. Plus, the last tactic was the one to fill the decks with bindings to that ended up not mattering. I had one deck more focused on tech cards with Goblin and Ultron, and another more on Undercover/Unleash with Yelena. Both benefitted from Star Lord's artifacts building up shards and draw. This was tougher than it looked and a really nice use of a Villains-style game with so many SHIELD heroes.

column36: I had never tried an Infinity Gems setup in Villains before but I thought it was good fun. The city stayed fairly full with the Gems sneaking back into the city but I prioritized no escapes so I managed to avoid that. I'm always a little unsure of how to handle "conversions" between Villains terms and heroic Legendary terms so I may have made the game harder on myself (for instance, not sending Hydra agents starter cards undercover with Dethlok cards because they aren't SHIELD) but I still enjoyed it.

Round one we had a lot of villains escape due to high villain count at the beginning that we couldn’t stop. round two we ended up getting cosmic threat and celestial Boon early, and it allowed us to crush the mastermind with ease. Nezarr The calculator is insane. Both games were fun.
I forgot to use the Celestial Boons in the first game.  Using them in the second game made it go a bit easier with the one that reduces the Mastermind Attack value.

This was definitely a challenging setup to try to get the best score, but it seemed very winnable. Both Black Panthers were incredibly helpful taking out the celestials, I just wish I got the Tiamut early.

Not a hard match to win. Winning with a low escape count seems like a different story!.I got away with three and felt lucky! 1st game I tried Solo and Captain Carter to try and make extra use of those half points but got a bad shuffle and it went horribly. Second attempt I upgraded to Old Man Logan and Gamora, DoT but still didn’t go great. Exitar escaped early on, which meant Henchmen were rare and city was under pressure throughout.

column36: This setup was a ton of fun. Ended up leaning into the discard engine with the Smash keyword to generate tons of card draw. This also led to rescuing tons and tons of bystanders with the 4 cost strength Angel. 1 hand was all we needed as it blew the whole game away and the other kinda spectated. We ended up playing just one attempt since it went so well and we were a bit crunched for time. As always, love the tourneys and can't wait til next time!

This game seemed very quick. I liked the way Colossus and Forged worked with Beast I was wanting to try that out with my build. Domino and Angel worked great together providing most of the force and ability to clear out everything.

Even with 5 heroes with excellent synergy we had to go to our second play for a good score.  Lots of fun with drawing multiple  cards and rescuing bystanders. The feasting of Angel;'s rare was a big setback but enabled to recruit and wallcrawl Beast's rare.  Imaginative  Set up.  Nice work!

Complete disaster in the first game with the absolute worst luck despite having a pretty solid Beast/ Forge/ Domino deck going for Player 1. Second game went okay. Getting Forge's BFG into play gave us a chance with 2 free hits on the Mastermind.

column36: Choosing Smash Heroes to pair with the Angel discard Common worked great! We were able to scale quickly and handle the MM. Our two escapes were both from rescuing the Copycat bystander with a full city.
It was a good combination of MM and scheme. We were unlucky with the hero deck but at least we had a good game.

Weirdly I enjoyed the first game (which I lost) more than the second, but both runs of these were awesome! Ended up losing game one after some HUGE pop-offs with Angel with just the face card left, but in Game 2 Angel and Gladiator Hulk played really nicely together, Angel's rare came out, and the tactics ended up pretty evenly ddivided between distinct strategies. This was a ton of fun!

In the second game I had Angel's rare plus several "discard 1 to draw 2" type effects, which was fun with Angel's green card - I was able to cycle my deck just about every turn toward the end, which is always a good time!

column36: Ghost Rider 2099 quickly becoming one of my favorites.  Cool combo with the +4 wound and Blade's Hunt High and Low.  Infernal Chainsaw go brrrrrrrrrrrrrrrrt.

I was fortunate: not many MS or ST were played and many of the villains haunted so the city was never under too much pressure. LH focused on weapon X sequence and tech/instinct superpowers (Fantomex, Weapon X, and Elsa) while RH focused on recruit / focus (Doom and Heralds). I was able to summon Galactus from the Hero Deck and use him twice to help defeat the Mastermind. Managing the HQ to minimize Romulus Weapon X Sequence was challenging and fun

column36: We really enjoyed this setup. My partner went with Elsa and Werewolf at night, using moonlight and instinct triggers to get going. I had a slower start but was building up a lot of recruit with the Heralds of Galactus. Eventually I was able to focus 11 into finding Galactus!! He came out hungry and ate 2 of the city spaces and the villains on them, and we were able to smack Romulus for the win.

Some interesting factors at setup - maximizing Tech and minimizing Weapon X sequences by trying to not choose heroes with 2 or 5 if possible to break up the chain... I imagine some brave souls will go the opposite and choose Fantomex and Weapon X to lean into it - but that just seemed to me to power up Romulus. This lead me to Brainstorm for Black and Super-Skrull for overall strength knowing we'd be "recruiting" heroes by fighting them. 

First run in and quickly apparent the Phalanx KO wasn't 'that' bad if it killed our troopers so going for Tech wasn't needed and Brainstorm was just too slow in a 6 hero deck with no other F4. So Hulk 2999 in from the bullpen to try and stave off the excessive master strike wounds. This worked better in being able to keep the city under control but still just felt too slow in turns. So interested in seeing if I was completely wrong and leaning into Weapon X sequence was the way to go instead!

I brought Psi-lord along because he had some good recruit. I wanted to attain and feed Galactush heaping portions of the city, I got him and started destroying the city. 
Once we got two uncommon heralds and the rare psilord and uncommon psi-lord we started drawing through the deck consistently with surfer. We found Galactus a couple of times leading to smashing that city, but he whiffed twice in full card draws without heralds so It could've been faster. 
Player two wanted to create a spiderman 2099 werewolf card drawing machine, which we got in game one but it developed late then and we couldn't save from the scheme. This time we missed out on that second covert undercover card so spiderman was bricking rather than flowing. So player two had smaller numbers leading to the much lower VP. 
Fun game though! It feels very exciting to nearly pull off the Galactus city crush. I was one city space away, but by then he was generating 14 attack alone, and the city was all 3-cost so Romulus was easy to slay.

column36: My friend who is new to Legendary ranked this setup a 5. That's probablly at least in part because he managed to rescue Groot's rare from Random very early in the game. That, coupled with a few of Star-Lord's artifacts and Jubilee's draw engine meant he stacked up shards and attack fast. I did ok with Banshee's Piercing Energy. We played a second game because he wanted to try new heroes, but that game went like 15 turns longer.
Ant man + Goliath + Legion is a great combo and Jubilee showed up much better than expected. General strat for picking heroes was to get tech and avoid x men

Went with Ant-Man and Jocasta to add some much-needed Tech into the mix.   Player 1 went with Lightshow primarily, which was not bad but wasn't near as effective as Player 2's Ant-Man/tech build.  Jocasta barely made an appearance, but got really lucky and only drew one Master Strike in 32 turns.  Game 2 I thought would go better with Rocket & Star Lord, especially since I got an early Tech artifact in play but despite some early success, it ended up going 4 turns longer so I'll submit Game 1.

Interesting Scheme that I've never played before.  Also noteworthy that last week I played Ultron Infinity, so going against Ultron 2 weeks in a row was fun.  I got some killer combos with Banshee and Jubilee's lightshow.  Legion's variety with having Piercing Energy and Lightshow on different cards made a huge difference.  

Amusingly, I had to play this setup 4 times.  1st Time I played on TTS and forgot to include Mandroids.  2nd' attempt I wrote down my stats on one of those scratch pads that have the button that erases the screen.  While my daughter invited a kid into the house and while going through my game room to the play area, he hit the button, so I had to play two additional times.  Same characters and strategy every time.  Left hand Banshee, and Right hand Jubilee.  The game I scored had a bit less Jubilee and a bit more Groot, but that was ok with me.

this felt like a long and stable game, 
there was quite some room to have less effective turns.
lightshow was fun to use, and i think my picks were decent.

column36:
First time in tournaments I could use everything (all bystanders incl stan lee). I did not really use many characters ofthe x men set, despite owning it for some time. So now i thought i will go (almost) full lightshow, even if it is suboptimal. 

Turned out to be a really fun game. Jubillee with the puercing energy card of dazzler was useful, helping to overcome ultron intimes he got overpowered by the onslaught symbiote. (First madtermind tactic gave 5 domination ot ultron, where i would have been stuck otherwise). Got 4 scheme twist in ditst 13 round, so city was peaceful but 8+1 masterminds ahead.  

From turn 21 left hand that went full lightshow could do a hit almost every other turn via piercing energy, sheer power, or woth some shard supplements from early groot card and groot rare (recruited via dazzler). Right hand was sort of a berserk deck, adter many ko and the northsar berserk cards. In the end it was also effective, so besides cleaning city it could hit the masterminds twice, including the final blow on turn 36.

I realized i like these longer games as well.

"Who is Onslaught?"
-Chuck Norris

OK setup. Nice to have a setup to use a lot of X-Men. Haven't played with it very much, so opted to try to maximize Lightshow instead of overthinking best hero choices too much. (Not in tourney contention anyways.) Felt like a "light" way to experience Onslaught. His static ability wasn't in play, and his masterstrikes didn't take effect until later in the game. Interesting challenge of the setup was that going Piercing Energy wasn't a slam-dunk choice because two of the villain groups were gainable heroes with no VP to fight. I feel very underwhelmed with Lightshow. Flavorfully, maybe fun to have Ultron absorb Hank Pym, but seems the whole pod doesn't own What If. Otherwise, it was fun to get a pod with X-Men and Civil War, particularly in the lower-half of the rankings.
Always like any setup with the Mandarin and the Ten Rings.
ehh not sure Mandarin rings are cool but I'd rather the henchmen just consistently KO.  Tried human torch but got too many wounds, Hiroim was definitely the best hero in the game.

column36: Transforming is so fun when it clicks!

Game one went pretty crazy, was koing everything with invisible girl focus from the start, and my right hand had 8 or 9 cards by the end with 3 rares and generated over 30 attack in one hand. I picked my heros to have some synergy with each other and outwit, as well as add tech heroes to hopefully avoid an unavoidable escape from mandarins tactic requiring tech, as no tech heroes are in the pod by default. While this did work, still had an early escape anyways before getting my deck up and running. Game two I just couldn't get anything to work, couldn't fight anything early, got wounds, and villains with locations piling in relentlessly. I honestly didn't even finish it, just want a good time. I think the setup was fine, but the scheme didn't super interest me, and nothing really felt exciting.
We enjoyed the scheme. We had some great combos going on with  She Hulk, Moon Knight and Humas Torch. Worked really well together.

column36: Multiple Man is my favorite, never-ending Clone brought me his entire hero set into one thin deck. And Omega Red's Wound trick didn't stand a chance with both copies of Reabsorb Duplicates in play each turn for a 2nd KO that turn. Felt like I made a good Hero choice with Falcon and Winter Soldier and Weapon H for a Berserk deck with Marrow, but it never really mattered.

Man that game shows once more how Devin and his team have been firing on all cylinders for the latest expansions. (Wish we could get some more....) Hulk 2099 was key to getting rid of wounds and I actually pulled Multiple Man off pretty well. Omega Red is interesting without being too difficult. The only downside is that the scheme was a real pushover for us. Never had more than 1 or 2 Villains in the HQ.

Push Pain Under The Surface from Hulk 2099 was great to circumvent Omega Red's returning wounds you KO, since they just go undercover instead, and then they power up his Rage Incarnate card.

I think I could have picked a better second hero than Fantomex in retrospect though, although he did help Marrow fire off her Berserk a few times.

Ravage 2099 is a super fun hero. I mostly ignored Multiple Man the first game, and used him but not very effectively in the second game.

column36: Great fun setup.  I was short on time this period and only got in one attempt.  Didn't even stress over choice of hero, just went suggested.  Would probably have subbed Squirrel Girl with Amadeus Cho had Iooked into it.  Very enjoyable setup.  I had one Singularity Investigator escape early on due to the annihilation wave.  Hand 1 got a great draw / smash engine going and never looked back.  Switcheroo helped build it.  Hand two just focused on the city with a decent approach.  100% would recommend this setup again.

The first game was an absolute train wreck. I'm convinced it was probably just a bagel shuffle. We were front loaded with twists and strikes. And Galactus came out and surprised us early. The second game was much better. It could have also just been a good shuffle. We had less strikes and twists in the first half, and Galactus came out later in the game. We had 3 escapes due to early villains having higher attack and not being able to hit (and a few lucky bystander pulls when the city was full), but after like the 10th turn or so, the city was more manageable. The switcheroo mechanic was very fun. That said, I am definitely noticing the setups in this particular tournament more challenging than the previous tournaments.

This was a weird one for us, we got rocked hard initially our first game, had a bunch of bystanders escape, and just seemed to get a bunch of twists/master strikes resulting in villains early. However, we got our decks going quicker, and took Annihilus down a lot faster. Our second one round we controlled the board really well, but it took us longer to get him down. We had an unlucky galactus show up and escape immediately which tanked round two, so we went with one.

I was dreading the veiled scheme. I honestly don't like all the searching and finding the new scheme mid match plus not having the flexibility to swap heroes if I play a full game is frustrating. Not to mention the actually VD stuff with Annihilus and Galactus. This is just a set up I shouldn't like but it came together. The first game I kinda got overrun quick. I was able to win but it was a dirty win. Round two I had a much better shuffle and was able to really let Mr. F shine with the last turn ranking up 30+ attack along with a full (large) deck draw only thing left over was 1 wound that I'd smashed.
Extremely fun setup as usual. We thought Psi-Lord was going to make everything pop off, very quickly realised he wasn't, but still won despite him/us.
